“How are you doing?” you ask, letting your gaze fall to her expectant belly. "+(amber.isCrew()?"Amber is sitting on a carpet":"The dryad is sitting by a tree")+", so you decide to sit down with her, wrapping an arm around her human midsection.
\n\n“Not very different, physically, but I’m kind of moody. And I’ve been getting hungry all the time. It’s a bit annoying. Anyway, sorry for complaining.” The deertaur’s face sinks a little.

\n\nYou give her a reassuring smile. “No problem, it’s good to get it out of your chest. By the way, I have just the thing to take care of that last problem,” you wink and pull out a carefully wrapped bundle out of your backpack. She eyes it curiously as you lay it down on the floor and pull the cloth open to reveal an assortment of delicious cookies. Picking another bundle, you add sandwiches to the menu, and a bottle of juice completes the "+(amber.isCrew()?"‘picnic’.":"picnic.")
\n\n“Oh my, you brought all this for me? It looks delicious!” she asks in a mix of surprise and delight. You nod with a smile, prompting her to hug you tightly for a moment.
\n\n“You’re pregnant, gotta make sure to get enough food,” you explain, giving her expectant deer belly a loving rub.

\n\nThe deertaur gives you a joyous grin. “Will do,” she declares and picks a sandwich, taking a big bite out of it. “Hmm, delithius!” she says with her mouth still stuffed, then washes it down with a swig of the juice.

\n\nYou chuckle and pick a sandwich for yourself. And so, you and the deertaur spend the next several minutes enjoying each other’s company. Eating takes precedence, and the only conversation that happens is some idle chit chat, but this simple act of wholesome closeness warms up your heart in a very comforting way.
\n\nOnce the last cookie is eaten, she licks her fingers in satisfaction. “I feel way better now! You really know how to treat a girl – I mean, not just in bed,” she winks.
\n\nYou chuckle. “To eat all that, you sure must have been hungry!”
\n\n“Oh, even if I wasn’t, this body still needs a lot of food, especially now that I’m expecting. Gotta make sure to nourish our baby... or babies! By the way...” she trails off with a demure look,

“I’m really thankful that you took me in. I never thought I would be able to leave that jungle and, you know, be treated as a person again. Never mind having kids! Sometimes, I think this is all a dream and I’ll wake up on the cold jungle floor.”

“I really appreciate that you took the time to visit, and not just to, you know, bang – but of course I love that too!” the dryad adds hastily, red-faced. “Sometimes I’m afraid you might go somewhere and never coming back.”
\n\nYou smile warmly. “I care about you, and not just because you’re the mother of my kids. I’ve liked you from the first time we met.”

\n\nThe deertaur smiles and gazes at you through bleary eyes, so you pull her into a reassuring hug. She wraps her delicate arms around you, and her loving touch makes you want to stay there forever. Seconds turn into minutes, until reluctantly you break the embrace and tell her you’ve got to return to your business. She nods with a smile, confident in your affection.
“Hey, how are you doing?”

\n\n“I’m doing fine. Even better now that you’re here,” she beams a smile, putting away a magazine about women’s health she had been reading before you arrived. Specifically, it’s an issue about pregnancy. There are more magazines and a couple of books in a pile by her side.
\n\nYou point at the magazine. “Doing some research?”

\n\n“Yeah. I mean, I’m gonna be a mother, so I figured I should try to learn all I can. It has lots of words I don’t know, but I’ve been managing with this so far,” she says proudly, pulling a dictionary from behind her."+(amber.isCrew()?"":" “I went to the gate and asked nicely, and eventually someone decided to help me out!”")
\n\nThat she managed to find a paperback dictionary in this day and age is nothing short of amazing, but you decide to talk about something else instead: “How’s the pregnancy going?”
\n\n“Way better than I expected, to be honest. Sometimes I get dizzy spells or nausea, but it lasts only a few minutes. And before you ask, yes, I’ve been making sure to eat well!” she chuckles.
\n\n“That’s good to hear,” you say, sitting by her side. “Uhh, can I?” you ask, pointing at her tauric midsection. It’s noticeably swollen.
\n\nIt takes a moment for her to understand what you’re getting at. “Oh! Sure, go ahead.”

\n\nYou place your hands on her soft fur and gently stroke it, feeling the contour of her pregnant belly. It’s not nearly as distended as a human’s middle would be, but the extra volume is there alright. After a few moments, you press an ear to it, hoping to find if your kid"+(e>1?"s are":" is")+" moving. Presently, it doesn’t seem like there’s any kicking, but you can clearly hear their heartbeat"+(e>1?"s":"")+". The sounds of the life you’ve helped to create makes your heart swell with a deep sense of pride and love.
\n\n“I think it’s more than one!” you say excitedly, prompting Amber to giggle happily.

\n\n“Hehe, I never thought I’d have someone feeling my belly like that, you know,” she starts. “It’s not half-bad, how can I explain? It makes me feel... loved,” she says, her eyes distant as she savors the word. “Hey, [pc.name], thanks for being a part of my life.”

\n\n“I actually had a little collection, but ever since I got like... this,” She gestures down at herself, “it’s been hard to keep track of them, so I’ve lost most by now.” She sulks a bit, but her expression lights up as she makes a realization. “...but I guess I can always start a new collection, right?”

“You know, we can have our kid"+(t+e>1?"s":"")+" watch them at the nursery. That way we won’t lose them. We can even paint some together, that’d be fun.”
